title: Finding
description: A single bugbot/scorer finding against a file in a repository. Lives in the shared schema set because both the harness (as a Scorer output) and bugbot CI (as the PR-comment payload) emit and consume it. `fingerprint` is a normalized-snippet hash so the same logical finding survives rebase / line-number drift.

confidence.description: Estimated probability that this finding is a true positive. UI surfaces this as a confidence bar; auto-fix policies gate on it.

category.description: Free-form category tag (`sql-injection`, `unused-import`, `data-race`, `secret-leak`). The harness's category taxonomy is open and extensible.

title.description: One-line headline for the finding. Rendered as the section header in PR comments.

description.description: Markdown-formatted longer-form explanation. May include code blocks, links, citations.

file_path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path to the file.

line_end.description: 1-indexed inclusive end line. Equal to `line_start` for single-line findings.

fingerprint.description: Stable hash of the (category, normalized code snippet, file basename) tuple. Used to dedupe across PRs and survive rebase / line-shift. Algorithm is implementation-defined but MUST be deterministic.

suggested_fix.description: Optional remediation. `patch` = unified diff; `replacement` = full file contents to overwrite; `note` = freeform guidance for a human to act on.

payload.description: The fix body. Format depends on `kind`.

evidence.description: References supporting this finding: related lines, trace span IDs, dataflow paths, prior commits. Each entry is an open object; concrete shape is bugbot-defined.

dismissed.description: Populated when a human dismisses this finding. Future runs that produce the same fingerprint MUST honor this dismissal until the suppressing config changes.

by.description: Dismisser identity — typically a username or service principal.
